name: Texas Round Steak
recipeIngredient: 1 lb 2 oz round beef (about 1/2 thick, weighed after fat and bone are removed)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, mix together the flour, salt, and chili powder.
2. Place the meat in a pie dish and dredge it in the flour mixture. Use about half of the mixture; you can discard the rest.
3. Heat oil in a heavy frying pan over moderate heat.
4. Add meat to the hot oil and brown on both sides. Transfer steaks to a 1 1/2 quart casserole dish.
5. In the same pan, add peppers and onions and fry over moderate heat, stirring frequently until softened.
6. Remove vegetables with a slotted spoon and spread them over the meat in the casserole dish.
7. Pour out any remaining fat from the pan.
8. Add beef broth to the pan and cook over moderate heat until it absorbs and browns the particles remaining in the pan.
9. Add tomato juice, garlic powder, ground cumin, and remaining ingredients to the broth. Mix well.
10. Pour the mixture over the meat and vegetables. Stir lightly with a fork to distribute the sauce evenly.
11. Cover tightly and bake at 325°F for about 1-1 1/2 hours or until the meat is tender.
12. Serve one piece of steak with some of the sauce per serving.
Food Exchange per serving: 1 FRUIT EXCHANGE + 3 LEAN MEAT EXCHANGE
CAL: 213
CHO: 9 gm
PRO: 20 gm
FAT: 11 gm
Low-sodium diets: Omit salt. Use low-sodium broth, tomato sauce and tomato juice.
